matched_objectives.name: Preserve digital evidence
matched_objectives.description: Ensure the integrity and authenticity of digital evidence is maintained.

matched_objectives.name: Store acquired data
matched_objectives.description: Store acquired data in one or more formats for subsequent examination and analysis

matched_objectives.name: Read data from digital evidence storage formats
matched_objectives.description: Access data within digital evidence containers such as disk images, memory dumps, or archive formats.

matched_objectives.name: Access device data for acquisition
matched_objectives.description: Connect to physical sources of digital evidence to facilitate data extraction

matched_objectives.name: Extract data from specific formats
matched_objectives.description: Process data to extract artifacts from specific data formats.

candidate_techniques.name: Write protect storage media using a hardware write blocker
candidate_techniques.description: A hardware write blocking device [should] prevent any change to data in the user area of a hard drive while allowing access to all data on a hard drive. (Lyle 2006)

weakness.name: Hardware write blocker fails to prevent modifications to the attached device.

mitigations.name: Thorough testing of write blocker against multiple targets to ensure that writes are not possible.
Regular checks for hardware write blocker firmware updates.
Subscription to notifications from write blocker vendor for firmware updates or identified problems.

weakness.name: Hardware write blocker hides the existence of an HPA.

mitigations.name: Testing to ensure that the software and hardware disk imaging setup can detect HPAs
mitigations.description: Tests have been performed that the combination of hardware and software used can detect when HPAs are present.

weakness.name: Hardware write blocker hides the existence of an DCO.

mitigations.name: Testing to ensure software and hardware setup detects DCOs

candidate_techniques.name: Write protect storage media using a software write blocker
candidate_techniques.description: Use of a software-based tool that prevents a computer from writing to computer storage media connected to it (NIST 2006)

candidate_techniques.name: Store bitstream data in a forensic image format
candidate_techniques.description: Storage of bitstream data recovered from a digital device

weakness.name: Data is written to forensic format that does not preserve the original raw data

mitigations.name: Check hash of image matches hash of source material

candidate_techniques.name: Store bitstream data in a raw image format
candidate_techniques.description: Storage of bitstream data recovered from a digital device as raw files.

candidate_techniques.name: Connect directly to storage media
candidate_techniques.description: This technique describes the occasions where it is necessary to connect directly to a disk without a hardware write blocker. This would typically then involve a subseqeunt software write blocker, which then presents a write protected interface.

weakness.name: Connection over a direct media interface causes changes to the target media

mitigations.name: Use software write blocker
Avoid direct connection to media and use hardware write blocker instead

candidate_techniques.name: Read bitstream from forensic image container file(s)
candidate_techniques.description: The decoding of a forensic format such as EWF such that the raw sectors can be accessed by a forensic tool (typically performed on-the-fly internally in a tool.)

weakness.name: Image format parsing fails to to present all blocks from within a forensic container image in their ‘flat’ (dd) representation
Image format parsing presents incorrect data within sectors of the image
Image format parsing presents incorrect forensic image metadata
Image format parsing fails to identify or assemble split forensic images

mitigations.name: Check hash of image matches hash of source device
Check hash of image matches hash of source material
Use a forensic image format with internal integrity and continuity metadata
Test the parsing of split forensic images
Test the parsing of split forensic images with the maximum number of segments possible
Test the parsing of split forensic images with missing segments
Ensure forensic tool UI clearly warns that there are problems while parsing a split forensic image file
Test the parsing of split forensic images with a corrupt segment

candidate_techniques.name: Extract metadata from within images
candidate_techniques.description: The extraction of metadata from within images (pictures). e.g. EXIF, XMP. IPTC, iTXt etc.

candidate_techniques.name: Access internal storage via bootable environment
candidate_techniques.description: Booting the suspected computer device into an alternative operating system in order to image the attached disks.

weakness.name: Failing to boot from intended forensic bootable environment, causing the normal system boot process to commence

mitigations.name: Use forensic bootable environment to gain access to internal media
Use a similar system to test steps required to interrupt the normal boot process and boot from Trusted Bootable Environment

weakness.name: Inability to load the forensic bootable environment due to system configuration
weakness.description: System configuration may not permit alternative booting e.g., Secureboot is enabled

mitigations.name: Use a Trusted Bootable Environment that is permitted to boot
Use USB flashdrive version if a CD version is not permitted to boot
Disable Secureboot in BIOS to permit bootable environment to load (risks wiping FDE key, requiring ADK).

weakness.name: Failure to detect internal disk from bootable environment
weakness.description: For example this may be due to missing drivers

mitigations.name: Use a Trusted Bootable Environment that supports accessing the internal disk

weakness.name: Failing to decrypt internal disk using the bootable environment

mitigations.name: Use a Trusted Bootable Environment that supports decryption of the internal disk
Acquire the encrypted contents of the internal disk and decrypt the copy within a virtualized environment

weakness.name: Bootable environment overwrites relevant data on the target device

mitigations.name: Use software write blocker
Test and document changes made by the tools used
Use tools only if changes made are to locations where relevant evidence is unlikely to be located.

workflow_guidance: 1. Confirm the objective(s) below match the investigative goal; use get_solveit_details(objective id) to see every candidate technique.
2. For each selected technique, review its error_mitigation_checklist (weaknesses are classified per ASTM E3016-18) and decide which mitigations to apply.
3. Record each executed step as a solveit-core:SolveitInvestigativeAction with usedTechnique and appliedMitigation, then validate the graph with validate_graph(extensions=['solveit']).
4. Optionally rate residual risk with solveit-wa:WeaknessEvaluation (likelihood x impact), as shown in the solve-it-investigation-planning recipe.
